title: GSD HANDOFF.json (v1)
$comment: Describes the 19-field HANDOFF.json contract emitted by bin/lib/checkpoint.cjs. Split: 17 upstream-compat required fields + 2 optional plugin-only extensions (source, partial). The 17 required fields mirror upstream GSD's /gsd:pause-work output verbatim (field names + types) so plugin output is consumable upstream and vice versa. The 2 optional fields are plugin additions from v1.1 Phase 4 (auto-compact checkpointing). `additionalProperties: true` leaves room for future plugin extensions without schema churn — strictness is on required-fields + types, not on an allowlist. See .planning/phases/08-handoff-schema-detector/08-RESEARCH.md for the upstream-subset analysis.

version.$comment: Schema marker. Pinned — if upstream ever bumps it, the validator surfaces the change.

timestamp.$comment: ISO-8601 timestamp of when the checkpoint was written (new Date().toISOString()).

source.$comment: Plugin extension (not in upstream). Optional for upstream-compat. Values: auto-compact (PreCompact hook), manual-pause (/gsd:pause-work or `checkpoint --source manual-pause`), auto-postool (PostToolUse hook periodic checkpoint added 2026-04-25 to bridge the microcompact gap; throttled by HANDOFF.json mtime to at most once per 60s).

partial.$comment: Plugin extension (not in upstream). Optional for upstream-compat. True when PreCompact's 5s budget ran out mid-gather; consumers should degrade gracefully.

task.$comment: checkpoint.cjs returns parseInt(n) || raw-string || null. Schema accepts all three.

completed_tasks.$comment: Each item is typically {id, name, status, commit?} but schema only enforces object shape — upstream allows flexibility.

uncommitted_files.$comment: Items not further constrained. checkpoint.cjs produces {status, path} objects; upstream shows [] as example. Schema stays permissive for consumer flexibility.
